description: A candid documentary color photograph (no on-image text or graphic overlay) taken during heavy snowfall on an airport tarmac. The large engine nacelle and lower fuselage of a commercial jet fill the left side of the frame; a de-icing truck/boom is at upper right. Two ground-crew workers in bright high-visibility yellow-green vests and dark cold-weather clothing work on the wet, snow-streaked apron -- one walking toward the camera, one crouched/working near the truck's wheels. Falling snow flecks the whole image, conveying harsh winter cold. It is a stock-style hazard photo, not a designed meme. It illustrates a DOL/OSHA winter worker-safety tweet ('How cold is too cold?') about protecting outdoor workers from cold stress. Intent is a public worker-safety PSA; the image itself carries no seal, slogan, or designed typography.
notable_text: (none on image; tweet text only -- 'How cold is too cold? Get tips on protecting outdoor workers in the winter')
